topic.name: Great Lakes Environmental Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting and managing water resources in the Great Lakes region.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to prohibit publicly owned treatment works from intentionally diverting waste streams to bypass any portion of the treatment facility if the diversion results in a discharge into the Great Lakes.
Requires notice to be provided to the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency prior to an anticipated bypass.
Document 2: Reauthorizes the Hydrographic Services Improvement Act of 1998 through FY2019.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt from the harbor maintenance tax cargo contained in intermodal cargo containers loaded at U.S. ports or ports in Canada located in the Great Lakes Saint Lawrence Seaway System.

topic.name: Rural Economic Development
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at enhancing economic opportunities and support for rural areas, including investments in housing, entrepreneurship, and assistance for individuals with disabilities.

Document 1: Rural Investment to Strengthen our Economy Act of 2007
expand the work opportunity tax credit to include a qualified small business employee
Document 2: require states to obtain the authorization of the individual with a developmental disability
Document 3: Low Income Housing Tax Credit Exchange Expansion and Job Creation Act of 2010
grants to state housing credit agencies in lieu of low-income credit allocations

topic.name: Oil and Gas Leasing Regulations
topic.description: Legislation governing the leasing, production, and environmental oversight of oil and gas resources on public lands and offshore areas in the United States.

Document 1: Fair Payment for Energy and Mineral Production on Public Lands Act
Deficit Reduction Through Fair Oil Royalties Act
Document 2: Clean Ocean and Safe Tourism Anti-Drilling Act
prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease for the exploration, development, or production of oil, natural gas, or any other mineral
Document 3: American Energy Independence and Price Reduction Act
competitive leasing program for the exploration, development, and production of the oil and gas resources on the Coastal Plain of Alaska

topic.name: Post Office Designations
topic.description: The documents focus on the official designation of United States Postal Service facilities in honor of individuals and the restructuring of judicial circuits.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 201 B Street in Perryville, Arkansas, as the 'Harold George Bennett Post Office.'
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 331 1st Street in Carlstadt, New Jersey, as the 'Staff Sergeant Frank T. Carvill and Lance Corporal Michael A. Schwarz Post Office Building.'
Document 3: Circuit Court of Appeals Restructuring and Modernization Act of 2007 - Amends the federal judicial code to divide the Ninth Judicial Circuit.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments on Consumer Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses recent legislative amendments aimed at modifying existing laws related to consumer rights, liability, and educational provisions.

Document 1: Repeals specified general limitations on liability for personal injury or death on seagoing vessels.
Addresses ecological and societal tolls caused by reckless errors.
Document 2: Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act to repeal the CFPB's authority regarding arbitration agreements.
Focuses on consumer financial products and services.
Document 3: Amends the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act to include expert witness fees in attorneys' fees.
Enhances provisions for civil actions under the Act.

topic.name: Prescription Drug Access Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to prescription drugs and regulating cost-sharing in health plans.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) from taking any action to implement any provision of the proposed regulation relating to part D of Medicare.
regulation relates to part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare)
Document 2: Establish cost-sharing limits for health plans that cover prescription drugs and use a formulary or other tiered cost-sharing structure.
Prohibits such a health plan from imposing higher cost-sharing requirements for specialty drugs than for other prescription drugs.
Document 3: Consumer shall have the right to choose to purchase prescription drugs from any domestic pharmacy.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to issue regulations that prohibit a pharmacy benefit manager (PBM) from providing incentives.

topic.name: Social Security and Retirement Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to social security benefits and retirement plans for older workers, including adjustments to benefit calculations and tax incentives for employers.

Document 1: Older Worker Opportunity Act of 2007 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow employers who maintain a tax-qualified pension or retirement plan...
Defines 'formal flexible work program'...
Document 2: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) of the Social Security Act to prescribe a formula for an emergency cost-of-living increase for Social Security benefits...
Document 3: Notch Fairness Act of 2015 - Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act to revise the formula for the computation of minimum Old Age Insurance benefits...

topic.name: Higher Education Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at improving student assistance and support for institutions of higher education.

Document 1: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
requires an IHE to disclose, prior to disbursement, the annual percentage rate applicable to a Direct Loan.
Document 2: Establishes in the Department of Commerce an Office of Minority Serving Institution Digital and Wireless Technology
strengthen the national digital and wireless infrastructure by increasing national investment in telecommunications and technology infrastructure at eligible institutions.
Document 3: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
extend the grace period before payment must begin on Direct Stafford Loans from 6 months to 12 months.

topic.name: Aviation Legislation and Funding
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative acts related to the funding and regulation of aviation and air traffic management in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Consolidated and Further Continuing Appropriations Act, 2013 to increase funding to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for air traffic organization activities.
Allocates certain appropriations for contract tower program for contract towers in operation as of February 1, 2013.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through August 1, 2010: increased excise taxes on aviation fuels and the excise tax on air transportation of persons and property.
Increases the authorization of appropriations for FY2010 for airport planning and development and noise compatibility planning projects.

topic.name: Law Enforcement and Victim Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing law enforcement capabilities and protecting vulnerable populations, particularly children and victims of hate crimes.

Document 1: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to extend the grant program for armor vests for law enforcement officers.
Document 2: Amends the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act to require a state plan for its child protective services system to include a certified assurance that the state has in effect and is enforcing a law requiring identification and assessment of all reports involving children known or suspected to be victims of sex trafficking.
Document 3: Authorizes the Attorney General to provide technical, forensic, prosecutorial, or other assistance in the criminal investigation or prosecution of any crime that constitutes a crime of violence under federal law or a felony under state, local, or Indian tribal laws.

topic.name: Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating fuel economy standards, tax incentives for energy production, and restrictions on financing high carbon intensity projects.

Document 1: Revises the Corporate Average Fuel Economy (CAFE) standards by requiring at least a 4% annual increase in the average fuel economy level
Requires manufacturers to ensure that 50% of automobiles and light duty trucks manufactured in model years 2013-2014 are dual fueled.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the excise tax credits for alcohol fuel, biodiesel, and alternative fuel mixtures
Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to reduce the corporate income tax rates by prescribing proportionate modifications to such rates based upon revenue savings.

topic.name: Labor Rights and Protections
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing the rights and protections of workers, particularly in relation to employment and reemployment rights, union organizing, and collective bargaining processes.

Document 1: Waives a state's sovereign immunity with respect to the enforcement of uniformed services members' employment or reemployment rights
Prohibits wage discrimination against members covered under USERRA.
Document 2: Requires the National Mediation Board to apply the same procedures to the direct decertification of a labor organization as are applied to elections to certify a representative.
Document 3: Sets forth special procedural requirements for reaching an initial collective bargaining agreement
Adds to remedies for unfair labor practices during union organizing drives.

topic.name: Veterans and Disability Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving healthcare access and support for veterans and individuals with disabilities.

Document 1: requires the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to furnish in-patient psychiatric care
closest VA facility or non-VA facility for veterans
Document 2: make physical therapists eligible for the National Health Service Corps Loan Repayment Program
Document 3: require state Medicaid plans to give individuals with disabilities the choice to receive care in a home and community-based setting

topic.name: Energy Infrastructure Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative measures and regulations governing the development and operation of energy infrastructure projects, including hydroelectric, natural gas, and nuclear facilities.

Document 1: Prohibits the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) from issuing a new license for a hydroelectric project on the Mohawk River in New York
Sets forth processing and approval procedures.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to permit the use of rights of way for natural gas pipelines through public lands

Deems the issuance of a sundry notice or right-of-way for a gas gathering line and associated field compression unit located on federal or Indian land and servicing an oil well to be an action categorically excluded for purposes of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 (NEPA).
Document 3: Authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to issue rules that designate no-fly zones in the vicinity of certain nuclear power plants
Prohibit the operation of aircraft in such zones.

topic.name: Gun Control and Drug Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to firearm possession and sales, as well as the regulation of marijuana use and penalties.

Document 1: prohibit the possession of a firearm by any person who has been convicted in any court of a misdemeanor sex offense against a minor.
Document 2: Prohibits the use of federal funds to require a person licensed to deal in firearms to report information to the Department of Justice.
Document 3: Prohibits the imposition of any penalty under an Act of Congress for the possession of marijuana for personal use.

topic.name: Consumer Data Protection Laws
topic.description: Legislation aimed at safeguarding consumer information and privacy from fraudulent access and misuse.

Document 1: Makes it unlawful to attempt to obtain, or cause to be disclosed to any person, customer proprietary network information (CPNI)
Prohibits the sale or other disclosure of CPNI obtained under false pretenses.
Document 2: Prohibits debt collectors from reporting a medical debt during a 120-day period if the consumer disputes the validity of such debt.
Amends the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act to protect consumer rights.
Document 3: Prohibits any financial or related institution from soliciting a Social Security number from an individual except for specific uses.
Prohibits the sale, provision, or display of Social Security numbers to the general public.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments for Benefits
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments to the Internal Revenue Code that enhance tax benefits for specific groups and activities.

Document 1: equalize and increase to $200 the tax exclusion for both transportation and parking fringe benefits
extends such benefits to federal employees
Document 2: exemption from income and employment taxes for real property tax abatements
provided services in exchange for such abatements
Document 3: reduce the maximum income tax rate on corporations from 35 to 25%

topic.name: Environmental Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on environmental protection, habitat restoration, and regional development initiatives.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to permit automatic expenditures from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund for annual shoreline erosion control projects
granting priority to projects containing a critical habitat as defined by the Endangered Species Act of 1973.
Document 2: adds the Tennessee counties of Hickman, Perry, and Wayne to the definition of 'Appalachian region' for purposes of Appalachian regional development efforts.
Document 3: establishes a nutria eradication program
provide financial assistance to Maryland, Louisiana, Delaware, Oregon, Virginia, and Washington to eradicate or control nutria and restore nutria-damaged wetland.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Human Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at promoting human rights, democracy, and freedom of expression internationally, particularly concerning internet freedom and trade agreements.

Document 1: promote the freedom to seek, receive, and impart information and ideas through any media
U.S. businesses operating in foreign countries should ensure online access to U.S.-supported websites and government reports
Document 2: prescribes requirements for mandatory sanctions with respect to Iran's human rights abuses
assist individuals and entities working in Iran for the purpose of supporting and promoting the rule of law, civil society, and economic opportunity
Document 3: review certain free trade agreements... including analyses of specified subjects and of each agreement, as well as whether the country has a democratic form of government that respects certain core labor rights and fundamental human rights
implementing bills of new trade agreements shall be subject to a point of order in the Senate unless such agreements include certain standards with respect to... fundamental human rights

topic.name: Federal Science and Technology Policy
topic.description: Legislation aimed at advancing research, education, and innovation in various scientific fields to enhance public health, energy solutions, and international cooperation.

Document 1: Expands the interagency oceans and human health research program
Authorizes development of new technologies for detecting and reducing hazards to human health from ocean sources.
Document 2: Establishes, revises, and extends specified science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) programs
Directs the Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) to establish a body under the National Science and Technology Council.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Energy to award competitive matching grants to consortia of institutions of higher education
Includes among such needs: the promotion of low-carbon clean and green energy and related jobs.

topic.name: U.S. International Diplomacy and Child Welfare
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative efforts related to international diplomacy, particularly in the areas of youth exchange, foreign service protections, and child welfare initiatives.

Document 1: United States-Poland Parliamentary Youth Exchange Program Act of 2007
establish a United States-Poland Parliamentary Youth Exchange Program.
Document 2: Foreign Service Line of Duty Death Gratuity Act of 2011
provide increased group life insurance benefits for a Foreign Service or government executive branch employee.
Document 3: Vulnerable Children and Families Act of 2016
promote oversight of the VCFS; addressing the gap in U.S. diplomacy, policy, and operations regarding permanent family care for children.

topic.name: U.S. Agricultural Policy
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative amendments and acts related to agricultural programs and support in the United States.

Document 1: Extends agricultural programs under the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002
Suspends permanent price support authorities
Document 2: Amends the Federal Agriculture Improvement and Reform Act of 1996
Repeals the sugar loan program
Document 3: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to make beginning farmer and rancher development grants available
Increases direct and operating agricultural loan limits

topic.name: Indian Trust and Sovereignty
topic.description: The documents discuss the management of Indian trust assets, the consultation processes with Indian tribes, and the transfer of land to support tribal sovereignty and governance.

Document 1: reaffirms the federal government's fiduciary responsibilities to Indians
establish an Indian trust asset management demonstration project that allows tribes to propose Indian trust asset management plans
Document 2: consultation with Indian tribes involves more than simply notifying a tribe
recognize and respect Indian tribal self-government and sovereignty
Document 3: requires the transfer of administrative jurisdiction over specified federal property
take such land into trust for the benefit of the Tribe

topic.name: U.S. National Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation concerning U.S. national security interests and international agreements involving military cooperation and oversight.

Document 1: Directs the Legal Advisor to the Secretary of State to provide Congress with the justification for the President's decision to deny Congress its constitutionally protected role.
Prohibits the use of funds to carry out such an agreement.
Document 2: Directs the Department of Defense to report to Congress annually on cooperation between Iran and Russia and how it affects U.S. national security.
The likelihood of Iran adopting the Russian model of hybrid warfare against potential targets.

Document 3: Authorizes the President to provide assistance to the government of Israel for the procurement, maintenance, and sustainment of the Iron Dome anti-missile defense system.

topic.name: Veterans Benefits and Regulatory Reforms
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving benefits for veterans and reforming regulatory processes affecting them.

Document 1: Allows the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to waive the $6 round-trip deductible paid by veterans in order to receive travel costs necessitated by required VA medical care
Requires the Secretary to revise the Veterans Health Administration Handbook to include such waiver periods.
Document 2: Amends the Regulatory Flexibility Act of 1980 (RFA) to revise the definition of 'rule' under such Act to exclude a rule pertaining to the protection of the rights of and benefits for veterans
Includes tribal organizations within the definition of 'small governmental jurisdictions' for purposes of such Act.
Document 3: Defines a 'covered civil action' as a civil action seeking to compel agency action and alleging that an agency is unlawfully withholding or unreasonably delaying an agency action relating to a regulatory action that would affect the rights of private persons
Requires an agency seeking to enter a covered settlement agreement to publish such agreement in the Federal Register and online not later than 60 days before it is filed with the court.

topic.name: IRS Oversight and Accountability
topic.description: Legislation focused on the oversight, accountability, and fair treatment of taxpayers by the Internal Revenue Service (IRS).

Document 1: Prohibits the IRS from developing or using any methodology that applies disproportionate scrutiny based on ideology.
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to expand the duties of the Inspector General for Tax Administration.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to treat professional employer organizations as employers for employment tax purposes.
Sets forth IRS certification requirements for PEOs.
Document 3: Prohibits the IRS Commissioner from providing performance awards until the IRS complies with document requests.
Holds IRS leadership accountable for transparency in investigations.

topic.name: Federal Budget and Funding Reforms
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming federal budget allocations and funding mechanisms for various programs and agencies.

Document 1: Amends the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act to limit penalties on states for reducing special education funding.
Redistributes federal special education funding denied to penalized states.
Document 2: Prohibits the use of an increase in guarantee fees to offset budgetary changes unrelated to business functions.
Restricts the Secretary of the Treasury from disposing of shares until specific legislation is passed.
Document 3: Allows unobligated budget balances to remain available for future expenditures.
Allocates part of the unobligated funds for deficit reduction.

topic.name: Health Insurance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at restructuring health insurance coverage and financing in the United States.

Document 1: Requires each adult individual to have the opportunity to purchase a Healthy Americans Private Insurance Plan (HAPI)
Establishes standardized coverage and state options for HAPI plans.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt from the excise tax on excess benefit employer-sponsored health care plans
Contributions to health savings accounts are included.
Document 3: Repeals the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
Provides for refundable tax credits for health insurance coverage.

topic.name: Health Policy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts related to health policy, including mental health services, drug application regulations, and abortion funding restrictions.

Document 1: Examining America's Mental Health Services Act of 2015
gaps in mental health treatment services for adults and children with severe and persistent mental illness
Document 2: Heart Disease Education, Analysis Research, and Treatment for Women Act
requires that a new drug application include any clinical data possessed by the applicant that relates to the safety and effectiveness of the drug involved by gender, age, and racial subgroup.
Document 3: Hyde Amendment Codification Act
Prohibits the expenditure for any abortion of funds authorized or appropriated by federal law

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: A collection of federal legislative acts addressing various aspects of government operations and public services.

Document 1: Amends federal law relating to information security
Requires federal agencies to perform an independent audit of their information security programs
Document 2: Directs each state that makes a Medicaid payment from federal funds
Requires an annual report to specified congressional committees
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Labor to consolidate government Internet portals
Authorize pilot projects to evaluate the feasibility of various portal options

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Security and Conservation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses U.S. legislative acts that address issues of national security, military detention, and environmental conservation related to specific species.

Document 3: Directs the President to restrict to a specified designated area the travel of officials and representatives of countries that are designated as state sponsors of terrorism
Authorizes presidential waiver of such restriction upon submission of a report to Congress.

topic.name: Transportation Regulations and Safety
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative and regulatory measures concerning transportation safety and operational guidelines for highways and railroads.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to update the FHWA's research report on reduced lighting on freeways.
Requires guidance to states on reducing lighting during low traffic density.
Document 2: Allows railroad employees to exceed hours-of-service requirements to clear blockages at grade crossings.
Document 3: Declares that the Surface Transportation Board shall not regulate liquefied petroleum gas rail transload facilities.

topic.name: Water Rights and Infrastructure
topic.description: Legislation related to the management, reclamation, and rights associated with water resources in various regions, particularly focusing on Native American tribes and local water districts.

Document 1: Amends the Colorado River Storage Project Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to create and operate a top water bank.
Requires the Secretary to expend specified sums for completion of the Navajo-Gallup water supply project.
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of projects to treat impaired surface water.
Limits the federal share of each of the project costs to 25%.
Document 3: Authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the Pechanga Settlement Agreement.
Confirms a Tribal Water Right of up to 4,994 acre-feet of water per year.

topic.name: Environmental Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating environmental practices, promoting clean energy, and ensuring public health regarding environmental contaminants.

Document 1: Directs the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to distribute among the states 1.5% of the emission allowances.
Requires states to use the proceeds from such sales exclusively for the benefit of consumers of home heating oil or propane.
Document 2: This bill amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to condition a state's receipt of funding from the Drinking Water State Revolving Fund on the state establishing a program to test for lead in school drinking water.
Testing must be conducted at least biannually at schools built prior to 1996.
Document 3: Amends the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978 to establish a combined efficiency and renewable electricity standard.
Advancing or encouraging clean, renewable, alternative, innovative and/or efficient energy technology, projects, research and/or practices.

topic.name: Child Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting children in various sectors, including entertainment and welfare services.

Document 1: Child Performers Protection Act of 2015
revises the application of child labor requirements and prohibitions with respect to child performers
Document 2: Child Welfare Provider Inclusion Act of 2015
prohibits the federal government from discriminating against child welfare service providers based on religious beliefs

topic.name: Immigration Reform and Employment Visas
topic.description: Legislation focused on creating pathways for immigrants to gain legal status in the U.S. while addressing employment needs and labor market demands.

Document 1: establish an employment-based, conditional immigrant visa (StartUp visa) for a sponsored alien entrepreneur
generate required levels of employment, revenue, or capital investment.
Document 2: confer 'blue card status' upon an alien who has performed agricultural employment
adjust a blue card alien to permanent resident status if the alien has fulfilled specified periods of agricultural employment.
Document 3: create a roadmap for immigrants who are here without legal status to earn citizenship
protect the sustainability of the American agricultural industry, including the dairy industry, with a stable and legal agricultural workforce.

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving educational assistance, healthcare advocacy, and recognition for veterans and their families.

Document 1: Post-9/11 educational assistance program
extend the age of eligibility of dependent children
Document 2: Ombudsman for Recovering Servicemembers
liaison for recovering servicemembers and their families
Document 3: POW/MIA flag must be displayed
displayed on all days on which the U.S. flag is displayed

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding temporary duty suspensions on specific goods.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain magnesium peroxide.
temporary suspension of duty on certain magnesium peroxide.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain single yarns of viscose rayon.
temporary suspension of duty on certain single yarns of viscose rayon.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain catalytic converter mounting and thermal insulation mats.
suspend temporarily the duty on certain catalytic converter mounting and thermal insulation mats.

topic.name: Government Appropriations and Authorizations
topic.description: The documents focus on the authorization and appropriation of funds for various government activities and programs.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2013 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2013
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011-FY2015 for construction of vessels to meet Navy force requirements
loan guarantees and commitments for the construction of new vessels
Document 3: Increases and extends through FY2010 the annual authorization of appropriations for Department of Veterans Affairs comprehensive service programs for homeless veterans

topic.name: Federal Communications Commission Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the regulations and policies of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) concerning media ownership, advertising decency, and telecommunications support programs.

Document 1: Amends the Telecommunications Act of 1996 to require the FCC to publish notice in the Federal Register for changes related to broadcast ownership.
Requires the FCC to establish an independent panel on increasing the representation of women and minorities in broadcast media ownership.
Document 2: Requires the FCC to revise the FCC's interpretations of regulations on indecent material for advertisements during specified hours.
Document 3: Prohibits the total amount of annual support provided through the Lifeline program from exceeding $1.5 billion.
Support provided through the Lifeline program may not be applied to mobile service if the service offering is limited to voice communications service.

topic.name: Election Reform Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at reforming electoral processes, including redistricting and campaign financing.

Document 1: Prohibits a state that has been redistricted after an apportionment from being redistricted again until after the next apportionment of Representatives
establishment by each state of an independent redistricting commission
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 with respect to eligibility and qualifying contribution requirements
establishment of a Fair Elections Fund
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 with respect to eligibility and qualifying contribution requirements
establishment of a Fair Elections Fund

topic.name: Educational Programs and Policies
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving educational access and quality for diverse student populations.

Document 1: Revises and reauthorizes programs under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA)
Requires states to adopt college and career ready academic content standards
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to make formula grants to states
Requires state grantees to designate a Coordinator for Education of Pregnant and Parenting Students
Document 3: Requires the Director of the National Science Foundation (NSF) to establish a nanotechnology in the schools program
Awarding matching grants to certain eligible institutions for the purchase of nanotechnology equipment and software

topic.name: Forest and Wildlife Management
topic.description: Legislation related to the management and conservation of forest resources and wildlife populations in the United States.

Document 1: Fulfilling Our Responsibility for Efficient and Sustainable Timber Supply Act of 2013
authorizes the Secretaries to enter into cooperative agreements and contracts with state foresters
Document 2: amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973
require the Secretary of the Interior to permit any state in which wolves are listed as an endangered or threatened species to conduct lethal and non-lethal control of wolves

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Risk Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating financial institutions and managing risks associated with investments and insurance in the financial sector.

Document 1: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 relating to prohibitions on proprietary trading
Prohibits any construction of these prohibitions to require the divestiture of collateralized debt obligations
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of the Treasury to guarantee eligible investments
Caps the maximum amount of investments by such a capital infusion fund at $50 billion
Document 3: Amends the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act of 2002
Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to consult with the Secretary of Homeland Security before certifying an act of terrorism

topic.name: U.S. Border and Transportation Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing security protocols and processes related to border control and transportation systems in the United States.

Document 1: urgent that the Transportation Worker Identification Credential (TWIC) application process be reformed
require the deployment of TWIC readers to ensure security
Document 2: expand resources, make grants, and take other actions to protect U.S. citizens and property
authorize the Secretary to make border security grants to local sheriffs' offices
Document 3: authorize the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security to determine whether checked baggage must be re-screened
requires the Assistant Secretary to report annually to Congress on the re-screening of baggage

topic.name: Food and Drug Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation and safety of food products and pharmaceuticals in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act to require testing for Shiga toxin-producing E. coli in beef.
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to implement sampling protocols for tracing contamination in meat products.
Document 2: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to remove dihydrocodeinone from classification as a schedule III controlled substance.
Requires a report on the impact of reclassification on patient access to pain management.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require harmonization of regulatory requirements for drugs and biological products.
Requires the Secretary to report on harmonized methods and approaches.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Construction and Defense
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures related to construction standards for federal projects and regulations governing defense contracting practices.

Document 1: Requires a commemorative work in the District of Columbia and its environs to be constructed using unmanufactured material mined or produced in the United States
Requires the Secretary of the Interior or the Administrator of General Services (GSA) to determine that the material to be used in its construction complies with such requirement.
Document 2: States that it is U.S. policy to recognize Jerusalem as the undivided capital of the State of Israel
Limits appropriations that may be obligated in FY2012 for Department acquisition and maintenance of buildings abroad until the U.S. Embassy in Jerusalem has officially opened.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Defense to debar from contracting with the Department of Defense (DOD) any defense contractor officially determined to have caused serious injury or death
Directs the Secretary to also debar any subcontractor found to have caused or committed such actions.

topic.name: Small Business Procurement Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing procurement opportunities and support for small businesses in federal contracting.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act, with respect to the SCORE program
repeal the program of assistance to microenterprises
Document 2: amends the Small Business Act to revise requirements with respect to solicitation of offers for bundled contracts
the Small Business Administration (SBA), with respect to either a single or a multiple contract award, must evaluate teams and joint ventures of small business concerns
Document 3: Increases from: (1) 23% to 25% the government-wide small business procurement contract goal
requires the total value of all prime contracts for a fiscal year to include the value of each contract awarded

topic.name: Veterans and Home Loan Refinancing
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and programs aimed at facilitating home loan refinancing for veterans and general borrowers through government-sponsored enterprises.

Document 1: maximum guaranty limit for refinance loans
percentage of an existing loan that the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) will refinance.
Document 2: Responsible Homeowner Refinancing Act of 2012
Home Affordable Refinance Program

topic.name: Land Conveyance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts pertaining to the conveyance of federal lands for various public and recreational uses.

Document 1: directs the Department of the Interior to convey specified land owned and managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Washington County, Utah, to the state of Utah
land shall be used only as an open riding area for the use of off-highway vehicles
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of General Services (GSA) to offer to convey to the Amy Biehl High School Foundation certain federal lands
Requires such conveyance to be in accordance with a fair market value appraisal
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Interior... to convey and assign to the District: all interest of the United States in all fixtures owned by the United States as part of the electric distribution system
Requires the District to assume all liability from the United States for the administration, operation, maintenance, and replacement of such electric distribution system.
